notes: These files are designed for rendering detailed type information, not just range checking.
Fields are intentionally verbose so the UI can choose what to show.
mainstreamSizes describes common ABIs/platforms; standardGuarantee describes what the language standard itself promises.
languageNotes: Go has fixed-width integer and float types plus architecture-dependent int/uint/uintptr and complex types.
Untyped constants in Go are conceptually arbitrary precision until a typed context requires representability.

types.summary: Fixed-width signed integer type with exact width 8.
specialRules: Conversion from larger numeric values truncates/sign-extends according to Go's defined conversion rules.

types.summary: Fixed-width signed integer type with exact width 16.
specialRules: Conversion from larger numeric values truncates/sign-extends according to Go's defined conversion rules.

types.summary: Fixed-width signed integer type with exact width 32.
specialRules: Conversion from larger numeric values truncates/sign-extends according to Go's defined conversion rules.

types.summary: Fixed-width signed integer type with exact width 64.
specialRules: Conversion from larger numeric values truncates/sign-extends according to Go's defined conversion rules.

types.summary: Fixed-width unsigned integer type with exact width 8.
specialRules: Conversions between integer types sign-extend or zero-extend to infinite precision and then truncate to fit destination size.

types.summary: Fixed-width unsigned integer type with exact width 16.
specialRules: Conversions between integer types sign-extend or zero-extend to infinite precision and then truncate to fit destination size.

types.summary: Fixed-width unsigned integer type with exact width 32.
specialRules: Conversions between integer types sign-extend or zero-extend to infinite precision and then truncate to fit destination size.

types.summary: Fixed-width unsigned integer type with exact width 64.
specialRules: Conversions between integer types sign-extend or zero-extend to infinite precision and then truncate to fit destination size.

types.summary: Semantic sugar for raw bytes, buffers, and binary data.
types.standardGuarantee: Exactly the same type as uint8.
specialRules: Alias, not new distinct defined type.

types.kind: alias of int32 / Unicode code point convention
types.summary: Conventional type for Unicode code points in Go source and APIs.
types.standardGuarantee: Exactly the same type as int32.
specialRules: Alias, not distinct type. Strings are UTF-8 byte sequences; rune is a code point concept, not a string element type with O(1) indexing in UTF-8 text.

types.definition: Signed integer type of natural machine word size.
types.summary: Default integer type in most Go code. Width is implementation-dependent but at least 32 bits, typically 32 on 32-bit targets and 64 on 64-bit targets.
types.standardGuarantee: Either 32 or 64 bits in mainstream implementations; exact width implementation-specific.
specialRules: Prefer int for ordinary indexing/counters unless exact width matters.
Do not assume int == int64 in portable code.

types.definition: Unsigned integer type of natural machine word size.
types.summary: Unsigned counterpart of int. Used less often in general Go than in C-family code; many APIs prefer int even for sizes/counts.
types.standardGuarantee: Either 32 or 64 bits in mainstream implementations; exact width implementation-specific.
specialRules: Unsigned arithmetic wraps modulo 2^N of the width.
Use carefully; mixing signed/unsigned is less idiomatic in Go than in C.

types.kind: unsigned integer large enough to store uninterpreted pointer bits
types.definition: Unsigned integer type large enough to store the bit pattern of a pointer.
types.summary: Low-level unsafe/runtime type, not a general-purpose substitute for pointers or sizes.
types.standardGuarantee: Width sufficient to store uninterpreted pointer value bits; typically same width as pointer size.
specialRules: Using uintptr breaks garbage-collector tracking if pointer liveness assumptions are violated.
Do not store uintptr long-term when you mean an actual pointer.

types.summary: Go floating type with 32-bit format semantics.
types.standardGuarantee: Float type corresponds to IEEE 754 binary32 representation category in the language specification.
types.rounding: Converting integer/float/complex values to smaller precision rounds to the destination precision as defined by the spec.
specialRules: Floating-point to integer conversion discards the fractional part (truncation toward zero).
0.1 is inexact in binary.

types.summary: Go floating type with 64-bit format semantics.
types.standardGuarantee: Float type corresponds to IEEE 754 binary64 representation category in the language specification.
types.rounding: Converting integer/float/complex values to smaller precision rounds to the destination precision as defined by the spec.
specialRules: Floating-point to integer conversion discards the fractional part (truncation toward zero).
0.1 is inexact in binary.

types.definition: Complex type represented conceptually as two float32 components.
types.summary: Built-in complex type for exact pair-of-floats complex arithmetic; width category 64.
specialRules: Real and imaginary parts follow the semantics of the corresponding float type.

types.definition: Complex type represented conceptually as two float64 components.
types.summary: Built-in complex type for exact pair-of-floats complex arithmetic; width category 128.
specialRules: Real and imaginary parts follow the semantics of the corresponding float type.
